name: Hajjaj ibn Masruq
title: Muezzin of the Caravan
shortDescription: The brave companion and muezzin of Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan who called the Adhan throughout the journey from Mecca to Karbala.
fullStory:
Hajjaj ibn Masruq al-Ju'fi was a renowned Shia and brave warrior from Kufa. He had the honor of being a companion of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). When he heard of Imam Hussain's (AS) departure from Medina to Mecca, he quickly went to Mecca and joined the Imam's caravan.

Throughout the journey from Mecca to Karbala, Hajjaj served as the 'Muezzin' of the caravan. At the stopping place of 'Qasr Bani Muqatil', he saw the tent of Ubaydullah ibn al-Hurr al-Ju'fi and, by the Imam's order, went to invite him to assist the Imam, but Ubaydullah declined this honor. Hajjaj's resounding voice in the Adhan of noon on Tasu'a and Ashura reminded everyone of the firmness and peace of the Hussaini caravan under siege.

On the day of Ashura, after getting permission from the Imam, Hajjaj went to the battlefield and fought with exemplary bravery until he was covered in blood. He returned to the Imam while blood was gushing from his body and recited a poem: 'May my soul be sacrificed for you, O guided guide; today I will meet your grandfather the Prophet (PBUH) and your father Ali (AS)'.

Imam Hussain (AS) replied: 'Yes, and I too shall meet them soon after you'. This glad tiding from the Imam created such eagerness in Hajjaj's heart that he returned to the battlefield with his wounded body and continued fighting.

After a long battle, he was finally martyred by sword blows while surrounded by the enemy. The Muezzin of Karbala, with his blood, called the Adhan of loyalty and love for Wilayah forever in history.
quoteArabic: أَلْيَوْمَ أَلْقى جَدَّكَ النَّبِيَّا، ثُمَّ أَباكَ ذَا النَّدَى عَلِيَّا
quote: (Bleeding, to the Imam:) Today I will meet your grandfather the Prophet and your father Ali (AS).
